title: 10 best herbal essential oils for ankylosing spondylitis
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for ankylosing spondylitis are a natural and holistic approach to managing the chronic inflammation and pain associated with this condition. They work by reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving joint mobility. The best essential oils for ankylosing spondylitis include Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Eucalyptus globulus, Salvia officinalis, Salix alba, Boswellia serrata, Ginkgo biloba, Cinchona officinalis, and Withania somnifera. If left untreated, ankylosing spondylitis can severely impact daily life, causing chronic pain, stiffness, and limited mobility, making everyday activities a struggle and affecting overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saffron essential oils helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce joint pain and stiffness. The oil's ability to penetrate deep into the joints and muscles also makes it effective in reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Additionally, saffron's natural analgesic and sedative properties can help to alleviate pain and promote a sense of calm, making it an excellent addition to a treatment plan for individuals living with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Saffron essential oil has potent anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and joint pain associated with AS, which is a chronic inflammatory condition.

Pain-relieving property: Saffron essential oil contains analgesic and sedative properties that may help alleviate chronic back and joint pain characteristic of AS, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.

Antioxidant property: Saffron essential oil's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to tissue damage and oxidative stress in AS patients, which can slow down disease progression and improve overall health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Saffron roots contain a high concentration of volatile compounds that help alleviate joint pain and inflammation associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Barks: Saffron barks have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing stiffness and improving joint mobility in patients with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh crocus sativus petals in late morning when they are fully open.
Dry the harvested petals using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours.
Steam distill the dried petals using a commercial steam distillation apparatus at 100°c for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil to remove impurities using paper towels and cheesecloth.
Store the final essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric essential oils helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compound, curcumin, works to reduce joint pain and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, turmeric essential oil's antioxidant properties help to combat oxidative stress and tissue damage, promoting healthy joints and reducing inflammation in the spine. As a result, turmeric essential oils may provide relief from the chronic pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis, improving overall quality of life for those affected by this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the joints, which is a key symptom of ankylosing spondylitis, and alleviates pain and stiffness.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of turmeric essential oil helps protect joints from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall joint health.

Cytokine-modulating: Turmeric essential oil's ability to modulate cytokines helps reduce inflammation by regulating the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting anti-inflammatory cytokines, which can help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Turmeric roots contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome, or underground stem, of the turmeric plant is rich in volatile oils, which are easily extracted to produce a medicinal essential oil that can be used topically to reduce inflammation and promote joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric root of curcuma longa by hand or mechanically at dawn when it is still dewy and has highest oil content.
Wash the harvested turmeric roots gently with cold running water to remove dirt and debris then soak in distilled water for 4-6 hours.
Steam distill the washed and soaked turmeric roots using a steam distillation apparatus with a ratio of 1:3 turmeric to water at atmospheric pressure.
Collect the distilled essential oil by condensing it into a clean glass container within 30 minutes of completion of the distillation process.
Filter the collected essential oil through a sterile coffee filter or paper towel to remove any impurities or debris and store in dark amber glass bottles for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood essential oils helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the chronic pain and stiffness associated with this autoimmune disorder. Additionally, wormwood's antioxidant properties may help to protect joints from further damage and promote healing. By incorporating wormwood essential oils into their daily routine, individuals with ankylosing spondylitis may experience significant relief from their symptoms.

Antioxidant: Wormwood essential oil's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that damage joints and contribute to the progression of ankylosing spondylitis, thereby reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall joint health.
Analgesic: Wormwood essential oil has analgesic properties that can help relieve chronic pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis, including back pain, stiffness, and reduced range of motion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce inflammation and promote healing in the joints.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in the early morning dew for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours or air-drying for 3 days.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a copper still with 10 liters of water at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel into dark glass bottles, resulting in 20ml of oil.
Store the essential oil in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months, shaking the bottle daily for maximum potency and shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose essential oils helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it possesses anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate symptoms of this chronic condition. The oil's natural compounds, such as beta-damascenone and geraniol, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the joints, relieving pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis. Additionally, damask rose essential oil has been found to improve range of motion and flexibility, making it a valuable adjunct therapy for managing this debilitating condition.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa damascena flowers in the early morning when dew is still present on petals for maximum oil content and potency.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 12 hours or air dry them for 24-48 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 250g of dried rosa damascena flowers with 500ml of high-quality carrier oil in a clean glass container and leave it to steep for 2-4 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids and retaining the infused carrier oil.
Transfer the infused carrier oil to a distillation apparatus, distill at atmospheric pressure, and collect the rosa damascena essential oil in a separate container after 2-3 hours of distillation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and stems from your garden or local nursery.
Wash the harvested plant material thoroughly with cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill 50 grams of the cleaned plant material using a still with a condenser for 30 minutes.
Collect the distilled essential oil by separating it from the water and storing it in dark glass bottles.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove any impurities before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which provide relief from chronic pain and stiffness in the spine. remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots at the peak of potency during late summer.
Dry the harvested roots in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Steam-distill the dried roots using 100ml of water per 30g of herbs for 2 hours.
Combine the essential oil with carrier oils such as coconut or olive at a ratio of 5%.
Bottle and store the finished essential oil in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

Collect the essential oil extract and mix with carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil in a 10:90 ratio.
Store the herbal urtica dioica essential oil blend in dark glass bottles with tight lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the joints, which is a major symptom of this condition. remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered root with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and stir well to mix.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool dark place for 14 days allowing the mixture to macerate and infuse.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Transfer the resulting herbal infusion to a distillation apparatus and vaporize using steam distillation to extract essential oil at 40-50°c temperature for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the joints. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves in abundance and air dry them slowly over several weeks to preserve potency.
Combine 100g of dried thyme leaves with 200ml of high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow for extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container, discarding solids, and set aside the extracted essential oil.
Allow the extracted essential oil to mature for 2-3 days before transferring it to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning when they are at their peak fragrance and oil content.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat ankylosing spondylitis with herbal essential oils is to combine a blend of lavender, ginger, and turmeric essential oils. Lavender oil helps reduce inflammation and promotes relaxation, while ginger oil alleviates pain and stiffness. Turmeric oil, rich in curcumin, reduces joint inflammation and improves mobility. Apply the blend topically to affected areas, or diffuse it in the air for a calming and soothing effect.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ankylosing spondylitis that are treated with herbal essential oils are conditions characterized by joint inflammation and stiffness, such as osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis. Certain herbs like turmeric, ginger, and frankincense have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of these conditions. Essential oils extracted from these herbs can be applied topically to the affected joints or inhaled to promote relaxation and reduce pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil contains curcumin, a compound that reduces inflammation and prevents the progression of the disease. It also has analgesic and immunomodulatory effects, which help alleviate pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis. Additionally, curcuma longa essential oil has been shown to improve joint mobility and reduce oxidative stress, making it a promising natural remedy for managing the symptoms of this chronic condition.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are key factors in the development of ankylosing spondylitis.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to reducing joint inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): It has potent anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative effects, which help in reducing inflammation and protecting the joints from damage in ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes are used to make essential oils for ankylosing spondylitis because they contain the highest concentration of curcuminoids, including curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties.

Stems: Turmeric stems, although less commonly used, are sometimes utilized for their essential oils, which may contain small amounts of curcumin and other beneficial compounds that help manage ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Steam distill 1 kg of dried curcuma longa rhizomes for 2-3 hours at 100-110 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separation device.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove any impurities or debris.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid at 5-10 degrees celsius.
Label the essential oil with its botanical name curcuma longa and the date of preparation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it contains compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active ingredients, such as gingerols and shogaols, work to reduce inflammation and pain in the joints, which are common symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis. By reducing inflammation and pain, Zingiber officinale essential oils may help alleviate the symptoms of this condition, improving quality of life and reducing the burden of chronic pain. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: 6-Gingerol: This gingerol compound has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Gingerol: Gingerol constituents exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis, including joint pain and inflammation.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and pain in joints affected by ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale for essential oils due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make essential oils for ankylosing spondylitis as they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of the condition.

Stems: Stems of Zingiber officinale are sometimes used to make essential oils for ankylosing spondylitis, although less frequently than rhyzomes and roots, due to their lower concentration of bioactive compounds, but still contributing to the overall therapeutic effect.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in late summer or early fall when the plants have matured.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a warm place with good air circulation for 1 to 2 weeks.
Steam distill the dried rhizomes using a ratio of 1 part rhizomes to 10 parts water for 2 hours.
Collect the resulting distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separatory funnel.
Store the extracted zingiber officinale essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it possesses anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties that can alleviate symptoms associated with the condition. Flavonoids (specifically, isoflavones): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate the progression of ankylosing spondylitis by reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy joint function.

Terpenoids (specifically, triterpenoid saponins): These saponins have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system's response to inflammation in ankylosing spondylitis, thereby reducing joint damage and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make essential oils for ankylosing spondylitis due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a well-drained area with full sun exposure.
Steam distill the roots in a distillation apparatus at 100-150 millibars pressure for 8 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it through a 10-micron filter.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 24 hours before using it in aromatherapy or skincare applications.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of eucalyptus globulus at dawn when the aroma is most potent and use 2 kg of foliage.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 pound of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of water for hydrodistillation.
Steam distill the mixture for 2-3 hours at 100-150 psi pressure.
Separate the essential oil from the water using a separator or decanting method.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove impurities and particles.

remedy_constituents: Salicin: Helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic agent.
Salicylic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate the symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis, such as reduced inflammation and pain in the joints and spine.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): Act as potent antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which is beneficial for managing the symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Barks: They are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve pain and reduce inflammation in the joints.
Stems: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antirheumatic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 kg of fresh salix alba leaves and twigs in the early morning to ensure maximum oil content.
Dry the collected plant material in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture.
Steam distill 1 kg of the dried plant material using a 1:3 plant-to-steam ratio for 2 hours to release oil.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a separator or decanter for 30 minutes.
Filter the collected oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove any impurities and achieve a clear consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata essential oils helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil's active compounds, boswellic acids, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to the disease's characteristic inflammation and pain. By reducing inflammation and alleviating pain, Boswellia serrata essential oils may help improve joint mobility, reduce stiffness, and enhance overall quality of life for individuals suffering from ankylosing spondylitis. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Aleuric acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce joint inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Boswellic acids (especially AKBA): They exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic activities, which help alleviate symptoms such as joint pain and stiffness in ankylosing spondylitis patients.

3-O-Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): This constituent has shown significant potential in inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Boswellia serrata are another commonly used part, as they also contain boswellic acids that contribute to the anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of the essential oil.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (or rhizomes) of Boswellia serrata are also used, as they contain a higher concentration of boswellic acids than other parts, making them a valuable component in essential oils for treating ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest boswellia serrata resin by cutting into the resin canals of mature trees and collecting the resin that flows out.
Steam distill the collected resin at a temperature range of 60-80°c for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the water phase through decantation and filter it using a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to prevent oxidation and preserve its quality.
Label the bottle with the date of production and the name of the essential oil and store it in a cool dry place for up to 1 year.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's ability to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress may alleviate the chronic pain and stiffness associated with the condition. Its neuroprotective effects also help to mitigate the neurological symptoms, such as numbness and tingling, that can accompany ankylosing spondylitis. Furthermore, Ginkgo biloba's potential to reduce inflammation in the joints may slow down the progression of the disease, improving overall quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to joints, which can alleviate pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Ginkgolides: They have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce joint inflammation and prevent tissue damage in the spinal joints, which is a common symptom of ankylosing spondylitis.
Bilobalide: It has antioxidant properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development and progression of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for ankylosing spondylitis.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba trees contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help alleviate pain and inflammation in individuals with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in the morning when they are still dewy and have a higher essential oil content approximately 50 grams.
Dry the leaves in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a distillation apparatus for 2 to 3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separating funnel and transfer it to a clean container.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains quinine, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and pain associated with the condition. The oil's ability to modulate the immune system and reduce oxidative stress also contributes to its potential benefits. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis has been found to have a positive effect on the body's natural ability to repair and rebuild connective tissue, which can become damaged in ankylosing spondylitis. This can lead to improved joint mobility and reduced symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Quinine helps by reducing inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis through its anti-inflammatory properties.
Quinidine: Quinidine helps by acting as a calcium channel blocker, which can help reduce muscle spasms and alleviate symptoms associated with the condition.
Cinchonine: Cinchonine helps by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cinchona leaves are used to make essential oils for ankylosing spondylitis due to their high quinine content, which helps reduce inflammation and pain.
Barks: Cinchona bark is used to make essential oils for ankylosing spondylitis due to its alkaloid content, including quinine, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Cinchona stems are used to make essential oils for ankylosing spondylitis due to their secondary metabolites, such as quinic acid, which may help reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis leaves and branches when the plant is in full bloom and the leaves are at their peak potency.
Steam distill the harvested cinchona officinalis material at a temperature of 100-110 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Collect the condensed steam which contains the essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter.
Separate the essential oil from the water and other impurities using a separating funnel or a centrifuge.
Bottle the extracted cinchona officinalis essential oil in dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera essential oils helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It has been found to reduce inflammation and pain associated with the condition by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Additionally, its adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can aid in managing the chronic pain and discomfort associated with ankylosing spondylitis. This may help improve quality of life and reduce symptoms, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroidal lactones have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Withaferin A: This lignan is known for its potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in the joints affected by ankylosing spondylitis.

Withanoside IV: This triterpenoid saponin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with ankylosing spondylitis, leading to improved joint health and reduced symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Leaves: They are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in affected joints.
Seeds: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help ease joint pain and discomfort in patients with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh withania somnifera roots and leaves in the early morning when the plant is dry.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 40 degrees celsius for 3 days.
Steam distill 250g of dried withania somnifera roots and leaves for 3 hours using a 2:1 ratio.
Collect the distilled oil and filter it using a paper filter to remove any impurities.
Store the withania somnifera essential oil in a dark glass bottle at 5 degrees celsius for up to 6 months.
